short_description: [P-2605860] Business Events Processing Stopped from 05:00 AM to 05:20 AM
close_notes:
Root cause:
The root cause is not explicitly identified in the provided incident details. The incident notes indicate that business events processing stopped, and a callout to L2 was raised to investigate and restart the affected application. However, the resolution activity mentions that business events 6 had not stopped processing, implying the issue may have been resolved without identifying a specific root cause. 

Resolution steps:
- Raised a callout to L2 to investigate the issue and restart the affected application.
- Monitored the situation, as indicated by the work note stating that business events 6 had not stopped processing, leading to the conclusion that the issue was resolved.

short_description: [P-2605852]  Failure rate increase from 6:44?PM UTC
close_notes:
Root cause:
The failure rate spike is caused by reservation search calls failing, with a majority of calls related to a specific basket. 

Resolution steps:
- Identified that the failure rate increase is due to reservation search call failures, particularly for a specific basket.

short_description: [P-2605847] No Booking with Payment from 09:30 AM to 09:45 AM
close_notes:
Issue Summary : No Booking with Payment for the hotels BRAMUL and COLALB.

Solution Provided : Hotel BRAMUL has no availability until October 1 due to a full refurbishment, as confirmed by Comm Ops. A test PayNow booking for arrival on October 2 was completed successfully without any issues, which aligns with the expected behavior. Comm Ops also confirmed that Hotel COLALB has been closed for bookings starting May 27. Hence both hotels, BRAMUL and COLALB, have been added to the exclusion list for the alert, and no further actions are required. The incident is therefore considered resolved.

Related Tickets : NA

short_description: [P-2605846] Marketing Access Token Error from 07:49 AM to 08:17 AM
close_notes:
Root cause:
Invalid token in Redis cache impacting the marketing-service-opera Kubernetes workload 

Resolution steps:
- Raised callout to L2 to clear the invalid token from Redis cache, enabling the system to fetch a new token
- Updated the cache after L2 intervention
- Closed the ticket following resolution

short_description: [P-2605843] On-Demand Jobs Finished Abnormally from 07:00 AM to 07:30 AM
close_notes:
Root cause:
On-Demand Refresh scheduled jobs finished too quickly, resulting in more than 50% of active jobs completing before 2AM UTC. 

Resolution steps:
- Ran On-Demand (OD) jobs for all affected dates to address the abnormal completion.

short_description: [P-2605839] [Distribution Problem] generic.server.exception in DC from 01:42 AM to 01:47 AM
close_notes:
Root cause:
A spike in Oracle response times and error rates led to generic.server.exception in DC availability. 

Resolution steps:
- Investigated the spike in Oracle response times and error rates observed around the time of the incident.
- Identified the correlation between the spike and the occurrence of generic.server.exception in DC.

short_description: [P-2605821] Response time degradation in github.com and distribution-config-server-service: EnvironmentController from 12:51 PM to 02:01 PM
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Response time degradation in github.com and distribution-config-server-service: EnvironmentController from 12:51 PM to 02:01 PM, 27th May.

Solution Provided : These errors occur because actuator refresh calls are failing intermittently when attempting to retrieve configurations from the EnvironmentController component of the distribution-config-server-service. Some failures are caused by unauthorized access errors, while others result from read timeouts due to slow response from github. Importantly, there is no impact on the customer journey. No further actions required and hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : NA

short_description: DE - Digital - PI.com -  guests informed us that they were trying to book on our website but couldn't. In one case, the credit card was even charged
close_notes:
issue summary :   guests informed us that they were trying to book on our website but couldn't. In one case, the credit card was even charged
solution provided : During investigation, we identified that on 26 May, reservations for Hotel Berlin City Wilmersdorf were not confirmed due to an error encountered while sending deposit folios for hotelId = BERWIL.
This led to an Opera room checkout error between 08:44 AM BST and 09:31 AM BST, during which PayNow reservations were not confirmed.
Under incident INC0664460, the HMS team implemented a workaround, after which the issue was resolved. Post-fix, test bookings for PayNow reservations were performed successfully, confirming that the hotel booking was functioning as expected
Parent incident : INC0664460

short_description: [P-2605765] No Booking with Payment from 8:30?AM UTC
close_notes:
Issue Summary : No Booking with Payment for the hotel SHEBAR.

Solution Provided :  All rates are restricted from May 26 to September 1 due to site maintenance, leaving the hotel SHEBAR under CTB with no resolution timeline yet. A PayNow booking test for SHEBAR hotel for arrival date of September 11 was successful. To prevent false alerts, SHEBAR will be added to the exclusion list until operations resume. Hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : NA

short_description: [P-2605740]  Failure rate increase from 1:48?AM to 2:21 AM UTC
close_notes: we observed failure rate is primarily caused by incoming requests targeting the invalid endpoint /gb/. Additionally, analysis shows that a significant portion of these requests originate from the client IP address 18.168.180.137, which is contributing to increase in failure rate.

short_description: [P-2605729] Marketing Access Token Error from 03:41 PM to 04:05 PM
close_notes:
Issue Summary : [P-2605729] Marketing Access Token Error from 03:41 PM to 04:05 PM, 25th May.

Solution Provided : We noticed increase in errors on marketing service between 03:40 ?PM to 03:50 PM BST, 25th May 2026. The errors were due to "CDH Exception - Unauthorized. Access token is missing or invalid." with a 401 response. As an agreed workaround, the faulty token was evicted from the Redis cache to mitigate the issue. Post which the errors stopped and the service failure rate was back to normal. No further actions required. CTECH-3878 has been raised for the fix from Engg team. Hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : CTECH-3878

short_description: [P-2605728] [Distribution Problem] Spike in Distribution Errors from 03:10 PM to 03:16 PM
close_notes:
Issue Summary : [P-2605728] [Distribution Problem] Spike in Distribution Errors from 03:10 PM to 03:16 PM, 25th May.

Solution Provided : During the issue window, the errors (Pre?Allocated Room is Not Ready, Kiosk Interface Not Currently Available -retry,  Invalid reservation numbers) that spiked were identified as acceptable and can be safely ignored in the alert query. We have now adjusted the alert configuration to ensure these business?known errors are excluded, preventing false alerts in the future. Hence resolving the incident.

Related tickets : NA

short_description: Price mismatch observed between SRP and HDP for multiple hotels and rate plans.
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Price mismatch observed between SRP and HDP for multiple hotels and rate plans on 22nd May.
Solution Provider:We noticed that there was price mismatches between SRP and HDP for the hotels LONSMI, GLASTA, CARNOR, LONARC, MANSPI, LONSTM. AvCache DB was not being updated with the latest updates and hence SRP prices were showing some mismatches. Further Ondemand job was run for all the 6hotels for 375days from today. Post which, the latest Opera prices (HDP) got updated to the AvCache DB prices (SRP). This resolved the price discrepancies. In the next occurrence, we will try to deep dive on the discrepancies. Hence resolving the incident.
Parent Incident:INC0541949

short_description: Delay in business events processing - 22nd May 2026
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Delay in business events processing - 22nd May 2026.

Solution Provided : From the ATE report, we noted that ~0.92M messages were triggered due to overnight ATE changes. During monitoring, delays were observed in streaming business events (Summary totals, Apply daily rates, Rate restrictions), which caused discrepancies between SRP and HDP and risked inconsistent pricing and availability. A solution has already been proposed under parent ticket INC0642990 / CTECH-9462, and we are awaiting a development fix from the respective team. As of 11:00 AM UK time on 25 May 2026, no delays in processing business events are observed. Hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : INC0642990 / CTECH-9462

short_description: Digital - Business - incorrect dates for bookings
close_notes:
Issue Summary : User is seeing incorrect dates for booking due to being abroad .

Solution Provided :  The booking AUZ6145689 was created with an arrival date of 16 May 2026 and a departure date of 17 May 2026 for the hotel London Hampstead (LONHMP). While all backend systems (Opera, CDH, payment APIs) correctly reflect these dates, the CCUI manage booking screen displays them based on the userDateTime field, which ties to the user's system timezone.  When bookings are made, the dates display correctly in the UK timezone (UTC+1), but when viewed from a US timezone (UTC?6) the dates appear shifted earlier by one day.  The issue is related to how the userDateTime field is being handled. This happens because the system is interpreting booking dates as full timestamps with timezone offsets, rather than fixed calendar dates. As a result, locations ahead of UTC (like the UK) show the expected dates, while locations behind UTC (like the US) show them preponed.

We have CTECH?5245 linked to parent incident INC0596374 for further triage and investigation.

Related Tickets : INC0596374 / CTECH?5245

short_description: FW: Fehlermeldung Premier Inn Berlin City Spittelmarkt (OBJId 681287)
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Since May 5th, CRC is not able to book for the hotel BERSPI via AMADEUS channel.

Solution Provided : The configuration was updated incorrectly in the hotels.yaml file, with the same ThreeCharCode (DCE) assigned to both hotels BERSPI and KOBCLE.
As a mitigation, a pull request was successfully merged to update the configuration for hotel BERSPI to use ThreeCharCode: DCA. Following this change, successful responses are now being triggered for BERSPI. This has been linked to the parent incident INC0658412 for further details.  Hence resolving the incident as confirmed by the caller over the teams chat.

Related Incidents : INC0658412

short_description: Hi Lauren, Hi Callum, I hope you both had a nice weekend.I am reaching out because we have a couple of points we would like to discuss in a call this week i
close_notes:
Root cause:
The functionality to turn upgraded room types on/off by user was broken, causing standard and upgraded room types to be returned. This resulted in partners booking these with VCC funds for the standard price, leading to payment failures on arrival. 

Resolution steps:
- The fix under CHG0047856 was successfully deployed on 22nd April, resolving the issue.
- Efforts are underway to extract bookings for affected partners (with upgrade room flag set to false) from February to 22nd April, though limitations in Dynatrace and Kibana restrict data retrieval beyond 20 days.
- Alternative extraction via OPERA is being explored, and the team is actively progressing with this and will share updates soon.
The data was finally exported from OHRA and shared with the caller

short_description: [P-260545] Drop in Bookings during Business Hours from 07:30 PM to 08:05 PM
close_notes:
Issue Summary : [P-260545] Drop in Bookings during Business Hours from 07:30 PM to 08:05 PM, 3rd May 2026.

Solution Provided : There has been a slight decline in bookings and revenue compared to the previous week, despite a higher number of user sessions. During the affected window (07:00–07:30 UK time), reservations this week totaled 1,328 from 22,235 sessions, whereas last week saw 1,541 reservations from 21,119 sessions. This appears to be driven by user behavior, as no significant errors or anomalies were detected across key service KPIs. Payment conversion remains strong, and all major services are performing without latency. Trends across payment types and options are consistent, with no irregularities observed. The website is functioning normally, and the Adobe team has not reported any concerns. Hence resolving the incident.

short_description: CUST-FEEDBACK: Digital - Unable to Payment using Credit Card
close_notes:
Issue Summary :  Unable to make Payment using Credit Card

Solution Provided :  We reviewed the CSQ recording and noticed that there were two booking attempts being made by the customer. The first (AUK1166003) for Dunfermline via the PI channel failed at the payment stage when using a Mastercard , due to an authorisation decline flagged as "SCA Requirer" (fraud check failure). The second attempt (AUK9825158) succeeded using a Visa card . A test booking with our Mastercard was successful, confirming the issue is specific to the user's card rather than the hotel or system. Hence resolving the incident as confirmed with Bryony.

- Assigned the incident to the digital team for further investigation
- Notified the pricing team and digital team to review the discrepancy
- Added Anja Vick (anja.vick@whitbread.com) to the incident watch list

short_description: PIB - Bookings / Reservation Management -  Error message appears when clicking
close_notes:
issue summary : Unable to amend booking in PIB and returns error
solution provided : Opera has a validation rule that limits the last-name field to a maximum of 22 characters, and spaces are counted as characters. In this case, the existing name totals 25 characters, which causes the validation issue. and returns error when clicking edit booking button in PIB

short_description: [CTECH-7036] Update deployment health check alert
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Discrepancy in the deployment counts (captured by the CE team alerts) between WANDA and HULK. 

Solution Provided :  As part of the recent deployment, SRE team introduced a new service, ddb-stream-basket-to-dt, in the HULK cluster. This service is designed to capture DynamoDB data and push it into Dynatrace as metrics, and by design, it runs only in HULK. Due to this cluster-specific deployment, a discrepancy was observed in the deployment counts between WANDA and HULK. To address this, the CE team excluded the service from alerts, and the SRE team deployed it in WANDA as part of CHG0048340. With these actions, the deployment counts are now aligned. Hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : CHG0048340
